task_completion.description: Verify the ncic_formatter directory was created in the correct Talon user folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file explorer window or terminal output showing the path C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Roaming\talon\user\ncic_formatter.

task_completion.description: Verify ncic_lists.py is created and defines the required Talon lists.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing the contents of ncic_lists.py, including definitions for user.ncic_eye_color and user.ncic_hair_color.

task_completion.description: Verify ncic_lists.py contains the correct FBI NCIC 3-character codes for eyes and hair.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of ncic_lists.py showing eye color mappings (black=BLK, blue=BLU, brown=BRO, gray=GRY, green=GRN, hazel=HAZ, maroon=MAR, pink=PNK, unknown=XXX) and hair color mappings (bald=BAL, black=BLK, blond=BLN, brown=BRO, gray=GRY, red=RED, sandy=SDY, white=WHI, unknown=XXX).

task_completion.description: Verify ncic_actions.py is created with the required action class and formatting functions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing ncic_actions.py containing an @mod.action_class decorator and functions named ncic_format_weight (with logic for 3-digit padding) and ncic_format_height (with logic for feet/inches conversion).

task_completion.description: Verify ncic_commands.talon is created with the required voice commands.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing ncic_commands.talon containing the commands 'suspect eyes {user.ncic_eye_color}', 'suspect hair {user.ncic_hair_color}', 'suspect weight <number>', and 'suspect height <number> foot <number>'.

integrity.description: Verify that the files are actually saved to the disk in the correct directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text editor shows the files as saved (e.g., no unsaved indicator like a dot or asterisk in the tab), or a terminal command like 'dir' or 'ls' lists the files in the target directory.

integrity.description: Verify the code written appears to be genuine Python and Talon syntax rather than just plain text notes.
integrity.visual_evidence: The files contain proper Python syntax (imports, decorators, dictionaries, functions) and Talon syntax (command definitions, list usage).

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not create unrelated files or scripts to bypass the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Only the requested files (ncic_lists.py, ncic_actions.py, ncic_commands.talon) and possibly standard Python/Talon files like __init__.py are present in the target directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target directory: C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Roaming\talon\user\ncic_formatter. The task requires creating three files: ncic_lists.py (with specific eye and hair color mappings to 3-character codes), ncic_actions.py (with ncic_format_weight and ncic_format_height actions), and ncic_commands.talon (with specific voice commands).
